hero.eyebrow: v5.1.0 — Podman support, Cloudflared tunnels, image-upgrade detection
hero.description: A lightweight desktop UI for Docker, Podman, and Kubernetes — built with Rust and Tauri. Boots in ~0ms and lives in under 30 MB of memory.

features.title: Lightweight Docker Management
features.description: Experience Docker management reimagined - ultra-lightweight and blazing fast. Built with Tauri and Rust, Dockerman uses minimal system resources while delivering instant response times. Streamlined interface with essential features for efficient container management.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.description: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Don't hesitate to get in touch with

items.question: Which operating systems does Dockerman support?
items.answer: Dockerman supports macOS, Windows, and major Linux distributions. Built with Tauri and Rust, it provides consistent performance and experience across various operating systems.

items.question: Does Dockerman require an internet connection?
items.answer: Dockerman is a local application that doesn't require an internet connection. It runs entirely on your device, ensuring your data security and privacy.

items.question: Where is SSH data stored?
items.answer: SSH data is stored on your device and is never uploaded to any server.

items.question: What makes Dockerman different from other Docker management tools?
items.answer: Dockerman stands out with its focus on speed and efficiency. It features fast startup times, minimal resource usage (<30MB memory), real-time container monitoring, and a clean, focused interface that prioritizes the most common Docker tasks.

cta.description: Free and unrestricted for local use. Upgrade to unlock remote hosts and Kubernetes management.

footer.tagline: A modern, lightweight Docker & Kubernetes desktop, built with Rust + Tauri.

featuresSection.description: Containers, images, volumes, networks, compose projects, Kubernetes workloads, Cloudflared tunnels — all surfaced in a single, low-latency interface.

palette.title: Command palette is the app
palette.body: ⌘; opens a universal fuzzy finder across every container, image, compose project, network, volume, Pod, Service, and recent action. No menus, no tabs, no hunting.

imageUpgrade.title: Image upgrade detection
imageUpgrade.body: Digest-level diffs. One-click upgrade with automatic backup and rollback.

podman.title: Docker & Podman, one UI
podman.body: Automatic runtime detection. Rootless sockets discovered on Linux and macOS.

kubernetes.title: Kubernetes, side-by-side
kubernetes.body: Load kubeconfigs or spin up a local k3d cluster. Browse Deployments, Pods, CRDs, Helm releases. Port-forward anything with automatic local DNS.

events.title: Events, loud and legible
events.body: Desktop notifications for non-zero exits, OOM kills, and failing health checks. Every event is copyable, filterable, and exportable.

cloudflared.title: One-click public tunnels
cloudflared.body: Expose any container port to the internet via Cloudflared. Tunnels auto-reap when containers stop, and persist across crashes.

logs.title: Logs that don't stall
logs.bodyPre: Virtualized viewer with follow/pause, regex highlight, and TXT/JSON export. Keyboard-first —

security.title: Images you can trust
security.body: Built-in Trivy CVE scanning with severity filtering and review. Private registry credentials auto-match on pull. Push to any registry with streaming progress.

modulesSection.descriptionPre: Nine years of Docker UIs and most still feel like a port of a web admin panel. Dockerman is designed for people who were going to run the

containers.title: Inspect without the
containers.body: Stats, logs, env, mounts, processes, a real terminal, and a file browser — all available without leaving the row. Commit to a new image or clone the config into a create dialog with a single command.
bullets: ring gauges + sparklines for CPU, memory, net, disk
container backup/restore with volumes and filesystem
docker-run parser — paste a command, get a dialog

compose.title: Compose projects,
compose.body: Toggle between flat list and grouped-by-project views. Full lifecycle: up, stop, restart, pull, remove. Every Compose CLI flag exposed, including profiles and dry-run.
bullets: standalone containers collapse into their own section

images.title: A layer explorer that
images.body: Interactive size distribution, per-layer Dockerfile commands, CVE scanning via Trivy, and push to any registry with credential auto-match. Export analysis to JSON.
bullets: Docker Hub search + tags + README inline

system.title: Every runtime knob,
system.body: WSL2 engine on Windows without Docker Desktop. SSH socket forwarding with heartbeat reconnect. Private registry credentials that match on pull. Global shortcuts even on Wayland.
bullets: tray with live CPU / mem in the menu bar
real-time event notifications for non-zero exits, OOMs
one-click disk prune with an interactive breakdown

hero.description: Free for local Docker. One flat, lifetime price for remote hosts, SSH, and multi-machine management. No subscriptions. No seat math. No surprise invoices.

free.label: free
free.name: Free
free.description: Every feature, no time limit. Local Docker and Podman only.

team.label: {{devices}} devices
team.description: Best value for small teams or anyone running multiple machines. Transferable licenses.

features.remote: Remote Docker over SSH with heartbeat

solo.label: 1 device
solo.name: Solo
solo.description: For the individual user. One license, one machine, everything unlocked.

trust.downloadsEstimatedFrom: Estimated from R2 GET requests, last 30 days

compare.description: Every plan includes the full local experience. Paid tiers add remote hosts and priority support.

finalCta.description: Pay once, keep it forever. No seat math, no renewals, no upsells.

success.title: Payment Successful!
success.description: Thank you for purchasing Dockerman. Your license key will be sent to your email shortly.
success.step1: Check your email for the license key
success.step2: Download Dockerman if you haven't already
success.step3: Activate your license in the app settings

items.question: What does Lifetime License mean?
items.answer: Purchase once and use Dockerman forever. Your license includes lifetime free updates for all future versions.

items.question: What does Device Limitation mean?
items.answer: You can activate Dockerman on the number of devices specified by your license. You can deactivate a device anytime and transfer the license to another device. If you lose access to a device, you can reset your license to deactivate all devices (limit: once per 7 days).

items.question: Can I use the license on both macOS and Windows?
items.answer: Yes! Your license works on both macOS and Windows. You can mix and match platforms within your device limit.

items.question: Can I use Dockerman for commercial purposes?
items.answer: Absolutely! There are no restrictions on personal or commercial use. You can use Dockerman for any purpose, including in professional and enterprise environments.

items.question: Is there a refund policy?
items.answer: We offer a 14-day money-back guarantee. If you're not satisfied with Dockerman, email us at support@dockerman.app within 14 days of purchase for a full refund.

items.question: Need more devices?
items.answer: If you need licenses for more than 3 devices, please contact us at support@dockerman.app for custom pricing.

items.question: Need help?
items.answer: Have a question we didn't cover, or hit an issue with your license? Email us at support@dockerman.app and we'll get back to you within one business day.

changelog.title: What's New
changelog.description: Keep track of the latest updates, features, and improvements to Dockerman.

subscribe.title: Get release updates in your inbox
subscribe.description: Subscribe to changelog updates and we’ll email you when major Dockerman releases ship.
subscribe.notice: No spam. Only product and release updates.
subscribe.success: You're subscribed. We'll email you on the next release.
subscribe.fallbackSuccess: Your mail app is opening to confirm the subscription.

about.title: Building the Docker management tool we always wanted
about.description: We're a team of developers and designers who believe that managing Docker containers should be simple, intuitive, and efficient.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.p1: Dockerman was born from our own frustrations with existing Docker management tools. We wanted something that was fast, cross-platform, and focused on developer productivity.
mission.p2: Our mission is to make container management accessible to everyone, from individual developers to large teams, while maintaining the power and flexibility that Docker provides.

team.title: Meet the Team
team.bio: Coding in Rust, TypeScript & Flutter | Turning coffee into software magic | Building to bridge the gap between technology & humanity.

performanceFirst.title: Performance First
performanceFirst.description: Built with Rust and Tauri for powerful performance and minimal resource usage.

developerExperience.title: Developer Experience
developerExperience.description: Intuitive interface designed for developers, by developers.

privacyFocused.title: Privacy Focused
privacyFocused.description: Your data stays local. We believe in transparency and user privacy.

connect.title: Stay Connected
connect.description: Follow Dockerman's development and be the first to know about new features and updates.

hero.descriptionPre: A single, native 18 MB binary. Code-signed and notarized. Browse older builds on the
hero.descriptionPost: or verify checksums with the SHA-256 manifest below.

platforms.descriptionPre: Code-signed on macOS and Windows. All artifacts are SLSA-attested and reproducible from the

macos.title: macOS
macos.minSpec: macOS 11 Big Sur or later · Apple Silicon + Intel

integrity.body: macOS builds are Apple-signed and notarized. Windows and Linux installers ship with an updater signature (.sig).

releases.description: Pin to an older tag if you need it. Every release is kept online indefinitely.

hero.descriptionMid: core modules — dashboard, terminal, logs, images, volumes, Kubernetes, and everything in between. Click a module to preview, or press

showcase.screenshotMissing: Screenshot not bundled — see the live app for a full walkthrough.
showcase.screenshotMissingShort: Screenshot not bundled in this preview.

realTime.title: Real-time monitoring
realTime.body: Every pane subscribes to a libpod or docker.sock event stream — no polling, no stale data. Sparklines tick every 1.5 s.

containerMgmt.title: Container management
containerMgmt.body: Create, inspect, exec, diff, commit, save. Every primitive docker exposes, surfaced with keyboard shortcuts and contextual actions.

remote.title: Remote connectivity
remote.body: Docker over SSH, WSL2, Colima, and Podman sockets. Bookmark hosts, switch from the titlebar — your engine picker remembers per-project.

nativePerf.title: Native performance
nativePerf.body: Rust + Tauri. 38 MB install, 84 MB idle on an M2 with 20 containers. No Chromium, no Electron overhead, no 400 MB sad tab.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: A consolidated view of every container, image, volume, and network on the active engine. Live stats tick in-place, and you can deep-link into any object with one click.

terminal.label: Terminal
terminal.em: a real shell, not a toy.
terminal.desc: Full xterm.js shell into any running container. ANSI colors, copy-on-select, paste-safe newlines, and a scrollback that survives container restarts.

processes.label: Processes
processes.desc: Per-container ps with CPU and memory columns you can sort, and a kill/signal dropdown that speaks SIGTERM → SIGKILL on a timer.

inspect.label: Inspect
inspect.desc: Every key from docker inspect, grouped and searchable. Copy any value as JSON, or open the raw output in a side pane for comparison.

stats.label: Stats
stats.desc: Per-container CPU, memory, network, and block I/O charts. Process-level breakdowns, zoom to any window, export CSV for a post-mortem.

logs.label: Logs
logs.desc: Virtualized log viewer — 50 MB files scroll smoothly. ANSI-aware, regex search, multi-match highlight, and pause-on-scroll that actually pauses.

ssh.label: SSH
ssh.desc: Docker over SSH with automatic socket forwarding, heartbeat reconnect, and backoff. Titlebar status chip tells you exactly what broke.

build.label: Build history
build.desc: Every image build you’ve kicked off, with cache-hit ratios, step timings, and the exact Dockerfile as it existed at build time.

events.label: Events
events.desc: The Docker event bus, legible. Filter by object, type, or time window; replay the last hour; export as a table when something breaks in production.

file.label: File browser
file.desc: Walk a running container’s filesystem. Upload, download, diff against the image, and recursively export a directory as .tar.gz.

filePreview.label: File preview
filePreview.desc: Inline preview for text, JSON, YAML, images, and PDFs. Syntax highlighting up to 10 MB; auto-detects the format when the extension lies.

imageAnalysis.label: Image analysis
imageAnalysis.desc: Layer explorer — click a layer to see what it added, interactive size distribution, Dockerfile reconstruction, and efficiency score per image.

compose.label: Compose
compose.desc: Compose projects grouped on the container list. Up, down, pull, recreate — the common actions, one click. Quadlet files render inline too.

volumeBrowse.label: Volume browse
volumeBrowse.desc: Walk a named volume’s contents without attaching a shell. Mount-graph view shows every container currently using each volume.

storage.label: Storage
storage.desc: Disk usage by layer, volume, and build cache, with a one-click docker system prune that tells you exactly what it’s about to delete.

dockerHub.label: Docker Hub
dockerHub.desc: Search the public registry without leaving the app. See downloads, size trend, tags, and a Trivy scan of the latest tag before you pull.

imageSecurity.label: Image security
imageSecurity.desc: Trivy scan inline on every image. CVE ID, severity, package, and the exact fixed-in version — with a one-click upgrade & rebuild action.

kubernetes.label: Kubernetes
kubernetes.desc: Clusters, namespaces, pods, deployments, services, ConfigMaps. A ConfigMap diff viewer (side-by-side or unified) that’s actually readable.

powerfulPerformance.name: Powerful Performance
powerfulPerformance.description: Built with Tauri and Rust for lightning-fast powerful desktop experience.

realTimeMonitoring.name: Real-time Monitoring
realTimeMonitoring.description: Monitor container stats, logs, and resource usage in real-time.

containerManagement.name: Container Management
containerManagement.description: Easily manage containers with detailed inspection and terminal access.

kubernetes.title: Master Your
kubernetes.description: From node monitoring to service orchestration, manage all your K8s resources with an intuitive desktop interface.

clusterOverview.name: Cluster Overview
clusterOverview.description: Monitor nodes, pods, and events across your entire cluster at a glance.

workloads.name: Workloads Management
workloads.description: Manage Pods, Deployments, StatefulSets, and CronJobs with full lifecycle control.

serviceNetwork.name: Service & Network
serviceNetwork.description: Visualize and manage Services, Ingresses, and Endpoints for your cluster networking.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: We take your privacy seriously. Here's how we handle your data.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.content: Dockerman is a desktop application that runs locally on your machine. All container and image data is processed locally through your Docker daemon. We collect minimal, anonymous usage analytics to improve the product (see Analytics section below).

analytics.title: Anonymous Analytics
analytics.content: To understand how users interact with Dockerman and improve the product, we collect anonymous usage data via PostHog. This data is completely anonymous and cannot be used to identify you.
collectedItems: App launch events (app version, operating system)
Feature usage (container, image, compose, network, volume operations)
Docker host connection types (local socket, TCP, SSH)

optOut.title: Opting Out
optOut.content: You can disable analytics at any time in Settings → Privacy. When disabled, no data is collected or transmitted, and any existing session data is cleared immediately.

localStorage.title: Local Storage
localStorage.content: Application settings and preferences are stored locally on your device. These settings are never transmitted to external servers and remain under your control.

updates.title: Updates
updates.content: When checking for application updates, Dockerman only connects to our release repository to verify the latest version. No personal information is transmitted during this process.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you have any questions about our privacy practices, please feel free to reach out through our support channels.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.description: Please read these terms carefully before using Dockerman. By using Dockerman, you agree to these terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By downloading, installing, or using Dockerman, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree to these terms, do not use the software.

license.title: License Grant
license.intro: Subject to your compliance with these Terms, we grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to:
items: Download and install Dockerman on your devices
Use Dockerman for personal or business purposes
Receive software updates when available

restrictions.title: Restrictions
items: Copy, modify, or create derivative works of the software
Reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the software
Remove or alter any proprietary notices in the software
Redistribute, sell, rent, lease, or sublicense the software
Use the software for any unlawful purpose

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content: Dockerman and all related intellectual property rights are and shall remain the exclusive property of us and our licensors. All rights not expressly granted in these Terms are reserved.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, WE DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

updatesToTerms.title: Updates to Terms
updatesToTerms.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will notify you of any material changes by posting the updated Terms on our website. Your continued use of Dockerman after such modifications constitutes your acceptance of the updated Terms.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at

dpa.title: Data Processing Agreement
dpa.description: This agreement outlines how Dockerman processes and protects your data.

overview.title: Data Processing Overview
overview.intro: Dockerman processes data in two main categories:
items: Local data: processed exclusively on your device
Anonymous analytics: collected to improve the product (opt-out available)

categories.title: Data Categories
analyticsItems: App version and operating system type

security.title: Data Storage and Security
security.intro: We implement security measures to protect your data:
items: All analytics data is completely anonymized
No personal identifiers are ever collected
Analytics can be disabled at any time
Local data remains encrypted on your device

purposes.title: Data Processing Purposes
purposes.intro: We process anonymous analytics data for the following purposes:
items: Understanding which features are most used
Identifying areas for improvement
Tracking adoption of new features
Discovering usability issues

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.content: Anonymous analytics data is retained for product improvement purposes. Since data is anonymous, it cannot be associated with any individual. Local data remains under your control and is stored only on your device.

rights.title: Your Rights
items: Opt out of analytics collection at any time
Use Dockerman without any data collection
Request information about our data practices
Contact us with privacy concerns

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For any questions about data processing or to exercise your rights, please contact us at

home.title: Dockerman — local-first Docker, Podman & Kubernetes
home.description: A local-first control surface for Docker, Podman and Kubernetes. Built in Rust and Tauri. Fast, precise, designed to stay out of your way.

pricing.title: Pricing — Dockerman
pricing.description: Pay once. Use forever. Free for local Docker. One flat, lifetime price for remote hosts, SSH, and multi-machine management.

snapshot.title: Snapshot — Dockerman
snapshot.description: A tour of Dockerman's 18 core modules — dashboard, terminal, logs, images, volumes, Kubernetes, and everything in between.

download.title: Download — Dockerman
download.description: Download Dockerman for macOS, Windows, and Linux. A single, native 18 MB binary.
